title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · Relational Lens: Dialect Portability
summary: A team must move a browser prototype toward PostgreSQL, justify any non-relational component, and defend the complete data system. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql dialect portability increment in a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
Record actor, authority, purpose, input provenance, query or change identity, and tamper-evident outcomes without logging secrets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability
steps.instruction:
theory case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 theory artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 3; justify any non-relational component.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dialect Portability contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate standard relational intent from sqlite and postgresql syntax, typing, null, date, and feature behavior. For Predict Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability, keep Dialect Portability connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 theory artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 3; justify any non-relational component.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Dialect Portability condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: PostgreSQL Concurrency Control and Indexes — Controls transaction isolation, concurrency phenomena, locking, serialization, index types, access paths, and operational tradeoffs.

hints: For Predict Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability, name the row grain, Dialect Portability precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability, trace this concrete input and predict rows or changes before running it: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 theory artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 3; justify any non-relational component.

For Predict Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability, challenge the documented misconception with result, constraint, or plan evidence: SQL behaves identically across all conforming databases.

options.text: Run a convenient Dialect Portability query for predict dialect portability, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid.
Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
SQL behaves identically across all conforming databases. That claim does not survive the predict dialect portability evidence boundary.

steps.title: Bound Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability
steps.instruction:
theory case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 theory artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 4; justify any non-relational component.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Dialect Portability application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate standard relational intent from sqlite and postgresql syntax, typing, null, date, and feature behavior. For Bound Dialect Portability · Theory Dialect Portability, keep Dialect Portability connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 worked example artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 3; justify any non-relational component. A defensible solution applies Dialect Portability, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio.

content.code:
SELECT ticket_id, status, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Record SQLite behavior, then verify syntax and semantics against PostgreSQL 18.4.
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: SQL behaves identically across all conforming databases. This fails the Dialect Portability contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: PostgreSQL Concurrency Control and Indexes (PostgreSQL 18.4 stable documentation) — Controls transaction isolation, concurrency phenomena, locking, serialization, index types, access paths, and operational tradeoffs.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.

options.text: Use Dialect Portability after naming its inputs, invariant or ownership condition, observable evidence, and failure boundary.
SQL behaves identically across all conforming databases. That claim does not survive the bound dialect portability evidence boundary.

steps.title: Repair the Dialect Portability defect · Theory Dialect Portability
steps.instruction:
theory case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 theory artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 2; justify any non-relational component.

A teammate acts on this belief: “SQL behaves identically across all conforming databases.” Which repair addresses the cause?

stimulus.title: Dialect Portability failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: SQL behaves identically across all conforming databases.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Dialect Portability, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the dialect portability defect evidence boundary.
Run a convenient Dialect Portability query for repair the dialect portability defect, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid.

steps.title: Build Dialect Portability evidence · Theory Dialect Portability
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-dialect-portability-1n23ix" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; dialect, storage-model, distribution, or production evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Dialect Portability evidence · Theory Dialect Portability.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Dialect Portability into observable performance evidence. For Build Dialect Portability evidence · Theory Dialect Portability, keep Dialect Portability connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-dialect-portability-1n23ix
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT typeof(owner_id) AS sqlite_runtime_type, COUNT(*) AS rows
FROM tickets GROUP BY typeof(owner_id) ORDER BY sqlite_runtime_type;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
